text:
Once upon a small village there lived a curious squirrel named Oliver. Each autumn his little nose twitched eagerly at the thought of collecting treasures.

This year wasn't different. Oliver scampered through the rusty autumn leaves searching for unusualities. That's when he stumbled upon an odd, triangular object. Its edges curled gently.

Investigating further, Oliver learned that this object was a woven spellbook. It was rumored that those fortunate enough to own a verse could speak trees. Oliver couldn't resist having a sip.

"I say unto these branches!" he declared. Suddenly a mighty oak leaned extra-deep.

"You favor us!" cried a voice.

"A squirrel!" Oliver crowded. The branches rustled.

Then he read aloud another verse:

"I speak of wildflowers! They dance under the moon."

A cluster of them popped up next to him.

"And I wish for peaceful mice…"

From then on, Oliver sprinkled his speeches with whimsical forest creatures.

From that day forth, Oliver wore his
